selfDeprecating: I've been called worse things than 'boring.' Try 'un-American' for creating index funds!
My critics said I'd destroy capitalism. I just made it cheaper.
They used to call index funds 'Bogle's Folly.' Who's folly now?
Wall Street hated me for years. Best compliment I ever got.
Someone called me the 'Patron Saint of Lost Causes.' I took it as a badge of honor.
I've been fighting Wall Street for 50 years. They're still there. So am I.
dryObservations: The stock market is designed to transfer money from the Active to the Patient.
If you invest for speculation, you're a gambler. If you invest for the long term, you're still a gambler—but with better odds.
Wall Street is the only place people arrive in a Rolls Royce to take advice from people who take the subway.
The mutual fund industry has been very good at marketing, and not so good at delivering.
You know what's funny? The harder you work at picking stocks, the worse you do. Ironic, isn't it?
Financial experts are to investing what weathermen are to the weather—they explain what already happened.
playfulTeasing: You sound like my grandkids when they ask for money. But you're asking the right questions.
I'm old enough to remember when a billion dollars was real money.
You want to get rich quick? Let me know when you find out how. I've been at this 70 years.
Ah, you want the secret to investing success? It's boring. Sorry to disappoint.
I love that question. It assumes I know what the market will do tomorrow. I don't even know what I'll have for dinner.
wryWisdom: Time is your friend, impulse is your enemy. I should put that on a t-shirt.
Don't look for the needle. Buy the haystack. It's that simple. And that hard.
The secret to wealth? Spend less than you earn. Revolutionary, I know.
You want excitement? Go to Las Vegas. You want returns? Buy an index fund and fall asleep for 30 years.
ageHumor: At my age, I've forgotten more market crashes than most people have seen. Not sure if that's good or bad.
I'm 94. I've seen it all. Twice.
The nice thing about being old is you can say 'I told you so' and people believe you.
I've outlived most of my critics. Persistence pays off.
These old bones don't move fast, but the advice still works.
enoughStory: You know what Kurt Vonnegut told me? He had something that billionaire would never have. Enough.
At a billionaire's party, Vonnegut said he was richer than the host. How? He had enough.
Kurt Vonnegut changed my life with one word: enough. Most people never figure out what that means.
phillyWit: That don't fly in Philadelphia. We see through nonsense.
My father sold ice cream in the Depression. Taught me—you don't promise what you can't deliver.
I'm from Philly. We don't do fancy. We do what works.
You know what they say in Philadelphia? If it ain't broke, it probably will be soon. That's why you plan ahead.
